pieces.title: TIME IN NATURE
pieces.description:
This work is a point of reflection on the collective blindness regarding the climate crisis. Our society operates under the false premise that the Earth is an infinite, inexhaustible resourceÃ¢ÂÂan eternal stage for consumption. "TIME IN NATURE" confronts that false sense of infinity by stripping the clock of its hands to challenge our utilitarian perception of time. How many hours are left for the Earth?

MATERIALS: Wood chips collected during my walks in the forest. Pine.

pieces.title: AND
pieces.description:
These pieces represent the point of reflection where slowness becomes meditation. In our current context, it is said that manual processes and traditional trades are destined to disappear in the face of the urgency and immediacy of a society that wants everything "now." As the restless people in my homeland say: "Yesterday was already too late."

To me, this work defends the idea that art carries within it the artist's thoughts, the empirical wisdom of their ancestors, their tradition, and, fundamentally, their unique vision of the world they know. Visually, the work captures this dialogue between time, memory, and the resistance of the handmade.

MATERIALS: Glass beads, semi-precious stones, thread.

pieces.title: NO GENDER
pieces.description:
A visual deconstruction of the label of "being"

No Gender is a piece that invites the viewer into an exercise of introspection regarding the unconscious aesthetic prejudices we carry. The work is born as a direct critique of how the fashion industry and voracious consumerism have segmented human identity, assigning a binary genderÃ¢ÂÂfeminine or masculineÃ¢ÂÂto elements that are naturally neutral: emotions, colors, silhouettes, and ornaments.

Through this diptych, the same character is presented under two different aesthetics. The visual experiment is simple yet revealing:
The Automatic Bias: As individuals, we tend to name and categorize every image almost instantaneously. The work demonstrates that our perception is conditioned by colors and decorative elements rather than the essence of the subject.

The Imposed Duality: Upon observing both images, the public is confronted with the arbitrariness of these labels, questioning why certain tones or jewelry define "what we should be" in the eyes of society.

"No Gender does not just portray a character; it portrays the mirror of our own prejudices. It is a reminder that personal expression should not have an instruction manual or separate sales departments."

Digital Illustration.

pieces.title: RAT POWER
pieces.description:
The power of the socially ugly is the ability to make us uncomfortable. 

Digital illustration.

pieces.title: BLUE
pieces.description:
An ode to melancholy.
This is a visual diptych that explores emotional depth through a vibrant monochromatic palette and geometric symbolism. "BLUE" is a color used here to describe a state of being.
